blurb: Christ's warning about stumbling blocks reveals the Father's protective heart—he would rather we enter life maimed than perish whole in our pursuit of what destroys. The radical surgery he prescribes is not divine cruelty but divine mercy, cutting away what we think we cannot live without so that we might discover what we truly cannot die without. Sometimes the most loving thing the Father does is make our cherished sins too costly to keep, not because he delights in our loss but because he delights in our life.

verses.text: Then said he unto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe unto him, through whom they come!
It were better for him if he had not been born, or if a millstone were hanged about his neck and he were cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones,
Take heed to yourselves: But if thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.
And if he trespass against thee seven times in the day, and seven times in the day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.
